title: Ukraine says it will open arms exports with 'Drone Deals,' but not to all countries
content: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday that Ukraine is set to open its arms exports via so-called drone deals, but not all countries will have access to Kyiv's battlefield tested expertise.
snippet: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday that Ukraine is set to open its arms exports via so-called drone deals, but not all countries will have access to Kyiv's battlefield tested expertise

title: Hungary's incoming PM Péter Magyar offers to meet Ukraine's president in June
content: Relations between the two neighbours hit rock bottom before Hungary's 12 April election that saw Magyar beat long-time nationalist premier Viktor Orbán.
snippet: Relations between the two neighbours hit rock bottom before Hungary's 12 April election that saw Magyar beat long-time nationalist premier Viktor Orbán.

title: European Parliament demands €200 billion extra for EU budget in hardball talks
content: The European Parliament has adopted its position on the EU long-term budget, calling for a 10% increase and pushing for EU-level taxes. MEPs say they are ready for tough talks as money talks intensify for 2028-2034 cycle.
snippet: The European Parliament has adopted its position on the EU long-term budget, calling for a 10% increase and pushing for EU-level taxes. MEPs say they are ready for tough talks as money talks intensify

title: German bid to rescue 'Timmy' the whale stranded off Baltic coast passes key hurdle
content: The whale was first spotted stuck on a sandbank on 23 March before freeing itself and then becoming stuck again several times.
snippet: The whale was first spotted stuck on a sandbank on 23 March before freeing itself and then becoming stuck again several times.

title: Ukraine says it shot down 33,000 Russian drones in March, a new monthly record
content: Ukraine has developed cutting-edge and battle-tested drone technology that has proved essential in holding back Russia’s bigger army and has drawn military interest from around the world.
snippet: Ukraine has developed cutting-edge and battle-tested drone technology that has proved essential in holding back Russia’s bigger army and has drawn military interest from around the world.

title: Ukraine summons Israel's ambassador over allegedly stolen grain being shipped to Haifa
content: Kyiv has warned Israel of a brewing diplomatic crisis, should authorities permit a vessel carrying grain allegedly stolen from Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories to dock and unload.
snippet: Kyiv has warned Israel of a brewing diplomatic crisis, should authorities permit a vessel carrying grain allegedly stolen from Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories to dock and unload.

title: Fact-checking claims that Spain's legalised migrants can move to other EU countries
content: Under Sánchez's programme, undocumented migrants who have lived in Spain for at least five months can apply for a one-year temporary residence permit, which has opened the door to disinformation.
snippet: Under Sánchez's programme, undocumented migrants who have lived in Spain for at least five months can apply for a one-year temporary residence permit, which has opened the door to disinformation.

title: UN human rights office rebukes Sweden for deporting disabled child twice to Albania
content: The committee, a body of 18 independent experts, monitors implementation of the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights by its state parties and conducts regular country reviews.
snippet: The committee, a body of 18 independent experts, monitors implementation of the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights by its state parties and conducts regular country reviews.

title: Italy extradites alleged Chinese hacker to US accused of spying for Beijing during COVID-19 pandemic
content: The FBI accuses Xu Zewei of hacking US government entities, research institutes and universities involved in vaccine research in 2020 and 2021 at the height of the pandemic.
snippet: The FBI accuses Xu Zewei of hacking US government entities, research institutes and universities involved in vaccine research in 2020 and 2021 at the height of the pandemic.

title: Pope Leo XIV prays with Archbishop of Canterbury in historic encounter at Vatican
content: The papal audience at the Vatican comes in the 60th anniversary year of the first formal ecumenical statement between the Anglican and Roman Catholic Churches.
snippet: The papal audience at the Vatican comes in the 60th anniversary year of the first formal ecumenical statement between the Anglican and Roman Catholic Churches.

title: Germany suspects Russia of Signal phishing attacks targeting politicians
content: Russia has often been accused of carrying out cyberattacks and acts of sabotage across Europe, allegations that Moscow has repeatedly denied.
snippet: Russia has often been accused of carrying out cyberattacks and acts of sabotage across Europe, allegations that Moscow has repeatedly denied.

title: Drone crashes in Romania as Russia strikes neighbouring Ukraine
content: Two British fighter jets were scrambled and 200 people were evacuated on Saturday after a drone crashed in Romania during a barrage of Russian strikes on its neighbour Ukraine.
snippet: Two British fighter jets were scrambled and 200 people were evacuated on Saturday after a drone crashed in Romania during a barrage of Russian strikes on its neighbour Ukraine.

title: Montenegro inches closer towards EU membership
content: Montenegro, which has been an EU accession candidate country since 2010, hopes to become the European Union's 28th member by 2028.
snippet: Montenegro, which has been an EU accession candidate country since 2010, hopes to become the European Union's 28th member by 2028.

title: EU and US deepen cooperation on critical minerals amid concerns over China’s dominance
content: EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič said that with the Memorandum of Understanding, "we will be delivering on our goals much faster than before."
snippet: EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič said that with the Memorandum of Understanding, "we will be delivering on our goals much faster than before."

title: EU leaders vow to boost security and economic ties with Middle East to minimise effect of Iran war
content: Leaders from Lebanon, Egypt, Syria and Jordan as well as the secretary general of the Gulf Cooperation Council, were in Nicosia to meet their European Union counterparts on the sidelines of an EU summit.
snippet: Leaders from Lebanon, Egypt, Syria and Jordan as well as the secretary general of the Gulf Cooperation Council, were in Nicosia to meet their European Union counterparts on the sidelines of an EU summ

title: Reigning two-time champ Carlos Alcaraz pulls out of French Open due to wrist injury
content: Alcaraz posted on X on Friday that he would also not play in the preceding Italian Open in Rome, where he also won last year.
snippet: Alcaraz posted on X on Friday that he would also not play in the preceding Italian Open in Rome, where he also won last year.

title: UK House of Lords accused of 'obstructionism' as time runs out on assisted dying bill
content: MPs in the House of Commons had backed legalising euthanasia for adults who have been given less than six months to live and can clearly express a wish to die, in a historic vote last June.
snippet: MPs in the House of Commons had backed legalising euthanasia for adults who have been given less than six months to live and can clearly express a wish to die, in a historic vote last June.

title: Judge sets trial date for suspects in Jewish charity ambulance arson attack in London
content: The UK has accused Tehran of using criminal proxies to conduct attacks on European soil targeting opposition media outlets and the Jewish community.

title: Europe revives renewables and nuclear to address the energy crisis
content: Brussels is looking for a long-term fix, putting both renewables and nuclear back on the table after the Strait of Hormuz closure sent energy bills soaring. Watch the video!

title: Magyar to meet von der Leyen as Hungary races to unlock EU cash
content: Incoming Hungarian Prime Minister lands in Brussels for what is described as "informal, but urgent talks" with Commission chief von der Leyen. Budapest is facing a tight deadline to unlock EU funding choked during the Orbán era.
snippet: Incoming Hungarian Prime Minister lands in Brussels for what is described as "informal, but urgent talks" with Commission chief von der Leyen. Budapest is facing a tight deadline to unlock EU funding

title: China hawks are gaining ground in the Commission. Will EU countries follow?
content: The European Commission's services are sizing up tools to counter Chinese rivals, including the Anti-Coercion Instrument. But despite mounting pressure from Beijing, the issue is still in its early stages — and EU member states remain divided.
snippet: The European Commission's services are sizing up tools to counter Chinese rivals, including the Anti-Coercion Instrument. But despite mounting pressure from Beijing, the issue is still in its early st

title: Brussels instructs EU countries to be tough on net-zero shipping goals at key IMO talks
content: The high-level talks come after a disastrous meeting in October, when a proposal to tax shipping emissions was blocked by the United States. Within the EU, Greece, Italy and Malta remain hesitant to support the proposal.

title: Louis Arnaud, former French hostage in Iran, describes Evin prison as 'the den of evil'
content: Speaking to Euronews, Louis Arnaud says the memories of Evin Prison continue to haunt him, as he recalls a place where captives are crammed into windowless cells and where the lights never go out.
snippet: Speaking to Euronews, Louis Arnaud says the memories of Evin Prison continue to haunt him, as he recalls a place where captives are crammed into windowless cells and where the lights never go out.

title: Ukraine-Israel row deepens over Russian shipments of stolen grain
content: In a continuation of the ongoing diplomatic uproar, Zelenskyy publicly criticised Israel on Tuesday for considering a purchase of Ukrainian grain stolen by Russia from temporarily occupied territories in southern Ukraine.
snippet: In a continuation of the ongoing diplomatic uproar, Zelenskyy publicly criticised Israel on Tuesday for considering a purchase of Ukrainian grain stolen by Russia from temporarily occupied territories

title: Polish-Belarusian journalist Andrzej Poczobut freed from Belarusian jail, officials say
content: Poczobut, a correspondent for the influential Polish newspaper Gazeta Wyborcza, was serving eight years in prison in a case condemned as politically motivated.
snippet: Poczobut, a correspondent for the influential Polish newspaper Gazeta Wyborcza, was serving eight years in prison in a case condemned as politically motivated.

title: EU 'ready' to sanction Israel over Russian vessel carrying stolen Ukrainian grain
content: A diplomatic crisis has erupted between Ukraine and Israel following the arrival in Haifa of a Russian vessel reportedly carrying stolen Ukrainian grain. Brussels says it is ready to sanction those involved.

title: European Parliament calls to define rape as sex without consent
content: A resolution adopted by the European Parliament calls for a common definition of rape across the EU, stating that any sexual act without consent should be considered rape.
snippet: A resolution adopted by the European Parliament calls for a common definition of rape across the EU, stating that any sexual act without consent should be considered rape.

title: What do we know about the viral claims of Iranian protesters facing execution?
content: US President Donald Trump claims he helped save these eight Iranian female protesters from execution, whilst social media users claim the image is AI-generated. What do we know about the women pictured?
snippet: US President Donald Trump claims he helped save these eight Iranian female protesters from execution, whilst social media users claim the image is AI-generated. What do we know about the women picture

title: Gunman arrested in manhunt after multiple shootings in central Athens
content: Police said a suspect armed with a shotgun initially opened fire at a social security office in the centre of the Greek capital, wounding an employee. He had a loaded revolver on him at the time of his arrest, according to media reports.
snippet: Police said a suspect armed with a shotgun initially opened fire at a social security office in the centre of the Greek capital, wounding an employee. He had a loaded revolver on him at the time of hi

title: 'It felt like the world was ending': One year after the Iberian blackout, is Portugal more cautious?
content: Portugal has proposed sweeping reforms following last year’s blackout, ranging from 72-hour power autonomy for key services to an independent alert system and an overhaul of SIRESP. Meanwhile, Euronews spoke to ordinary Portuguese about their country being swept up by darkness.
snippet: Portugal has proposed sweeping reforms following last year’s blackout, ranging from 72-hour power autonomy for key services to an independent alert system and an overhaul of SIRESP. Meanwhile, Euronew

title: Which European countries have the most teenage entrepreneurs?
content: Around 69,000 EU entrepreneurs are under 20. The Netherlands and Italy lead the pack — a positive sign for Rome, amid gloomy youth employment rates.
snippet: Around 69,000 EU entrepreneurs are under 20. The Netherlands and Italy lead the pack — a positive sign for Rome, amid gloomy youth employment rates.

title: Watch: Champions League semi-finals: When football meets global capital
content: From Qatari-backed PSG to fan-owned Bayern and US-financed Atlético and Arsenal, the Champions League semis double as a battle of club ownership models.
snippet: From Qatari-backed PSG to fan-owned Bayern and US-financed Atlético and Arsenal, the Champions League semis double as a battle of club ownership models.

title: Violence against women 'evolved in Europe', says MEP Al-Sahlani ahead of Parliament's vote on rape
content: MEP Abir Al-Sahlani urges EU countries to pressure the Commission to propose an EU-wide definition of rape. On the war in Iran, the MEP says "global order is being renegotiated in real time".
snippet: MEP Abir Al-Sahlani urges EU countries to pressure the Commission to propose an EU-wide definition of rape. On the war in Iran, the MEP says "global order is being renegotiated in real time".

title: Malta's Prime Minister Robert Abela calls early general election for May
content: The Maltese leader mainly cited rising energy costs due to the Middle East conflict, as concerns and fears of a spike in inflation grow in a country reliant on imports as reasons to seek a new mandate.
snippet: The Maltese leader mainly cited rising energy costs due to the Middle East conflict, as concerns and fears of a spike in inflation grow in a country reliant on imports as reasons to seek a new mandate

title: EU energy mix: Crisis pushes balance on nuclear, renewables and security
content: The EU is pivoting back to nuclear after decades of hesitation, driven by the dual shock of the energy crisis and the realisation that renewables alone can't power a heavy industrial economy.
snippet: The EU is pivoting back to nuclear after decades of hesitation, driven by the dual shock of the energy crisis and the realisation that renewables alone can't power a heavy industrial economy.

title: Merz suggests Ukraine’s EU membership may depend on territorial concessions
content: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said on Monday that Ukraine’s proposed timeline for EU membership is unrealistic, as debate over enlargement continues to intensify. “Zelenskyy had the idea of joining the EU on January 1, 2027. That will not work. Even January 1, 2028, is not realistic."

title: Euronews explains: Can a country ever be suspended from NATO?
content: Can an ally be really expelled from NATO by another country? What is the procedure, and can Donald Trump really dictate the terms of the alliance? Euronews explores the legality and the politics behind NATO.

title: Norway and France launch joint police probe into diplomats' alleged links with Epstein
content: According to Norwegian media, police are investigating financial assistance the couple received to purchase an Oslo apartment in 2018 at a price below market value.
snippet: According to Norwegian media, police are investigating financial assistance the couple received to purchase an Oslo apartment in 2018 at a price below market value.

title: US lawmakers urge EU not to water down deforestation law ahead of review
content: After meeting US Ambassador to the EU Andrew Puzder on Monday, Environment Commissioner Jessika Roswall said the top priority is to ensure the bloc’s anti-deforestation rules address the global challenge of deforestation, while continuing to support implementation and provide further clarification.

title: French prison guards strike over overcrowding as jails near 90,000 inmates
content: French prison guards blockaded facilities nationwide over overcrowding and staff shortages, as unions demand emergency hiring in a system nearing 90,000 inmates.
snippet: French prison guards blockaded facilities nationwide over overcrowding and staff shortages, as unions demand emergency hiring in a system nearing 90,000 inmates.

title: Defence: Which European countries have mandatory and voluntary military service?
content: Countries across Europe are reviving conscription amid shrinking military numbers and heightened geopolitical tensions worldwide. Can mandatory military service fill the gaps?
snippet: Countries across Europe are reviving conscription amid shrinking military numbers and heightened geopolitical tensions worldwide. Can mandatory military service fill the gaps?

title: Germany sees surge in conscientious objectors amid new conscription law
content: Germany has seen a spike in conscientious objector applications after new conscription laws, as debate over military service and security continues to intensify.
snippet: Germany has seen a spike in conscientious objector applications after new conscription laws, as debate over military service and security continues to intensify.

title: Watch: Pentagon's leaked email: Can Spain be suspended from NATO?
content: A leaked Pentagon email suggested punishing Spain and even reconsidering UK-backed Falklands support, exposing deep NATO rifts over a possible Iran war. Can it be done?
snippet: A leaked Pentagon email suggested punishing Spain and even reconsidering UK-backed Falklands support, exposing deep NATO rifts over a possible Iran war. Can it be done?

title: The UN should 'reform, transform or die', says former General Assembly President

title: Israel and Hezbollah finding ways to go back to "pre-ceasefire modus operandi" - Lebanese Minister

title: Nuclear, renewables, and green hydrogen: Is the EU betting on the right energy mix?
